skill.name: explorer
skill.description: Find files, symbols, definitions, and code locations quickly without making edits or deep design changes.
skill.summary: Fast codebase navigation with concise file references and next inspection targets.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography and reconnaissance findings are false positives. The flagged lines contain descriptive navigation guidance only, with no code execution, cryptography, network access, or data exfiltration instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:3, but this line is a plain English description of searching and navigation. No evidence found of a weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line contains only skill metadata prose. There is no code, hash name, encryption logic, or instruction to use weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:26, but this line advises avoiding deep design changes. No evidence found of a weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is task-scope guidance for an exploration assistant. It does not mention cryptography, hashing, ciphers, or security-sensitive computation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md:26 as reconnaissance, but the line limits the skill to navigation and context. No evidence found of host enumeration, credential discovery, or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is intended to locate code references within a user-provided codebase. The file also disables write and edit tools, reducing abuse potential.

content.value_statement: Large repositories make it hard to find the right files and symbols quickly. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward concise codebase discovery.
actual_capabilities: Locates relevant files, symbols, and definitions in a codebase.
Summarizes findings with concise file references.
Suggests next files or symbols to inspect.
Keeps analysis focused on navigation and discovery.
Runs with write and edit tools disabled by configuration.
limitations: It is not designed for deep architecture review.
It does not edit files or apply fixes.
It depends on available codebase search tools.
It does not replace full security or performance analysis.

use_cases.title: Find an Entry Point
use_cases.description: Locate the files that start a feature, command, route, or workflow before deeper analysis.

use_cases.title: Trace a Symbol
use_cases.description: Find definitions and related references for a function, class, component, or configuration key.

use_cases.title: Prepare Review Context
use_cases.description: Collect the most relevant files and next inspection targets before a code review.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the main files related to this feature and summarize why each file matters.

prompt_templates.title: Locate a Symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Find where this symbol is defined and where it is used. Include concise file references.

prompt_templates.title: Map a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace this workflow through the repository. List the key files in the order I should inspect them.

prompt_templates.title: Build Review Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a focused exploration summary for this change area. Include related files, likely dependencies, and next symbols to inspect.

output_examples.input: Find the files that define the login flow.
output_examples.output: A concise list of authentication files, each with a short note explaining its role in the login path.

output_examples.input: Where is the billing status calculated?
output_examples.output: The likely calculation file, related model or service references, and suggested next symbols to inspect.

output_examples.input: Prepare context for reviewing a routing change.
output_examples.output: A focused review map covering route definitions, handlers, middleware, and adjacent tests.

best_practices: Start with a specific feature, symbol, route, or workflow.
Ask for file references and short notes together.
Use the suggested next files to continue investigation.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for full implementation work.
Do not ask it to make edits or refactor code.
Do not expect complete security conclusions from navigation output.

faq.question: Does this skill edit code?
faq.answer: No. Its configuration disables write and edit tools.

faq.question: What is the main purpose of this skill?
faq.answer: It helps locate files, symbols, definitions, and related code quickly.

faq.question: Can it perform deep architecture analysis?
faq.answer: No. The skill is optimized for fast discovery, not deep design review.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static findings were reviewed and no confirmed security issue was found.

faq.question: What should I provide in a prompt?
faq.answer: Provide a feature name, symbol, route, command, or workflow that needs exploration.
